articles.title: NO, REALLY, BASH IS NOT ENOUGH: WHY LARGE-SCALE CI NEEDS AN ORCHESTRATOR
articles.summary: While Bash scripts work for simple CI needs, they are not enough for complex, large-scale CI pipelines because they don't have inherent orchestration for parallel jobs, dependencies, and error handling. Mature engineering teams therefore require a dedicated CI orchestrator that provides job isolation, a comprehensive control plane for visibility, and structured primitives to define complex, distributed build pipelines.

articles.title: BENCHMARKING HOW WORKFLOW EXECUTION SCALES ON POSTGRES
articles.summary: A single Postgres server can sustain 144K raw writes and process 43K durable workflows per second, with WAL flushing and table lock contention as primary bottlenecks. While performance can handle billions of daily operations for most applications, sharding is a good option for achieving even higher loads.

articles.title: BUGS RUST WON'T CATCH
articles.summary: A 2026 audit of the Rust-based `uutils` project found 44 security vulnerabilities caused by logical errors during operating system interactions, rather than memory safety issues. While Rust successfully prevented all memory-related bugs, developers must still use defensive programming patterns to handle complex system behaviors securely.

articles.title: I CAN NEVER TALK TO AN AI ANONYMOUSLY AGAIN
articles.summary: Advanced AI models, particularly Claude Opus 4.7, have an uncanny ability to identify authors from small, even unpublished, text excerpts by detecting subtle stylistic "tics." This capability might be the end of online anonymity for frequent writers, as traditional protections are expected to disappear.

articles.title: LEARN ALGORITHMS FOR INTERVIEWS, FORGET THEM FOR WORK
articles.summary: Technical interviews often emphasize theoretical algorithmic puzzles that rarely appear in production environments where standard libraries predominate. Real-world engineering instead demands solving complex, ambiguous problems through design trade-offs and incremental delivery of value.

articles.title: MANAGE CLERK FROM YOUR TERMINAL WITH THE NEW CLI
articles.summary: Clerk's CLI is here and open source. clerk init detects your framework and scaffolds auth into your project. clerk config manages sign-in methods and session policies in code. clerk api queries users, organizations, and sessions. Install via npm, pnpm, bun, yarn, or

articles.title: CLICKY
articles.summary: Clicky is an AI buddy for Mac that provides on-screen guidance and automation for any task you're working on.

articles.title: ROCKY
articles.summary: Rocky is a Rust-based control plane that serves as a trust system for data pipelines, with features like compile-time contract enforcement and automated schema drift recovery. It provides a suite of tools for management, including precise lineage tracking and integration with Dagster and VS Code.

articles.title: THE PULSE: TOKEN SPEND BREAKS BUDGETS – WHAT NEXT?
articles.summary: Rapidly increasing AI token expenditures are causing huge budget overruns, prompting companies to implement stricter measurement and usage limits. While some organizations continue to prioritize innovation through a "let it rip" strategy, many are now actively curbing costs by transitioning to more affordable models and local alternatives.

articles.title: THE COMPOUNDING SOFTWARE FACTORY
articles.summary: AI reverses the natural degradation of engineering teams by forcing code hygiene and maintaining solid institutional knowledge. These processes help managers especially with faster feedback loops, allowing teams to consistently produce higher-quality work at a faster pace.

articles.title: YOU CAN BEAT THE BINARY SEARCH
articles.summary: A novel approach was introduced to efficiently search sorted arrays of 16-bit integers, focusing on improving the traditional binary search algorithm. This new method is the "SIMD Quad algorithm" and it integrates a quaternary interpolation search with SIMD parallel processing, and consistently outperforms standard binary search.

articles.title: TIRED OF SEARCHING FOR THE ROOT CAUSE OF EVERY WEIRD TOKEN SPIKE WHEN YOU SWITCH MODELS?
articles.summary: This workshop will explain how Sentry Agent Monitoring cracks open the AI black box to tell you what broke. Get costs down and

articles.title: performance up without the headache. Save your spot
GEMINI FREE TIER IS ALL YOU NEED
articles.summary: The Gemini free tier is enough for low-volume, non-critical personal automations and prototypes.

articles.title: MICROSOFT OPEN-SOURCES "THE EARLIEST DOS SOURCE CODE DISCOVERED TO DATE"
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced the earliest discovered source code for 86-DOS, the foundational operating system that predates its acquisition and development into MS-DOS.

articles.title: PU.SH
articles.summary: Pu.sh is a minimalist coding-agent harness written in 400 lines of shell script.

articles.title: ON THE FUTURE OF APPLE'S VISION PLATFORM
articles.summary: Apple will not abandon the Vision Pro even though it has had disappointing sales.
